title: USD Foundational Data Types Capability
purpose: Define the implementation-neutral catalog and value-shape rules for USD foundational data types.

included: semantic aliases and alias agreement with underlying types
array type naming and element constraints
dictionary value shape and recursive dictionary combining
native value storage without retained adapter JSON
stored type identity inspection and checked typed reads
native numeric payload storage for scalar, dimensioned, and array numeric values
complete typed payload catalog coverage for scalars, dimensioned values, matrices, quaternions, semantic aliases, arrays, dictionaries, and value blocks
deferred: full authored list operation algebra, owned by usd-listops-authored
path-valued specialized types, owned by usd-paths
spline, reference, payload, retiming, relocate, variant, and time sample specialized types
value resolution, interpolation, asset resolution, and schema fallback evaluation
half-precision arithmetic and rounding behavior beyond preserving half type identity and readable payloads

required.reason: The token scalar is a foundational type, but its registered-string identity and constant-time equality/hash expectations are owned by usd-tokens.

not_required.reason: Foundational type names are fixed spec names, not parsed identifiers. Identifier scanning belongs to parsers that consume textual syntax.

normal.note: normal direction; unit length is advisory

container_types.array: Append [] to a scalar, dimensioned type, or semantic alias whose underlying type is scalar or dimensioned. Arrays may be empty. Arrays of opaque, including opaque semantic aliases, are not permitted.
container_types.dictionary: Unordered map of string keys to scalar values, dimensioned values, arrays, or dictionaries.
container_types.listop: Foundational list operation element types are int, int64, uint, uint64, string, and token. Algebra is owned by usd-listops-authored.

storage_model.json_materialization_rule: Tagged JSON objects are adapter and serialization output only. Durable foundational values and parser raw values must not retain Json payloads as their authoritative representation.
storage_model.dictionary_storage_rule: Dictionary values are stored as typed native entries and materialize the tagged JSON dictionary shape only when canonical output is requested.
storage_model.payload_storage_rule: Raw and durable values must preserve one observable active USD value type and payload. The physical storage shape is implementation-defined and may be std::variant, tagged union, enum-plus-payload storage, arena-owned values, or a backend-native value container.
storage_model.payload_catalog_rule: Raw and durable values must cover the complete foundational type catalog as typed payload categories: each scalar type preserves its scalar domain and precision/width; each dimensioned type preserves element type and shape; matrix values preserve matrix shape/order; quaternion values preserve component order; semantic aliases preserve authored alias type, semantic role, and underlying type agreement; arrays preserve homogeneous element type, element shape, and alias metadata; dictionaries preserve typed entries; value blocks preserve a distinct sentinel.
storage_model.typed_read_rule: Values expose stored type identity and checked typed reads without materializing or reparsing adapter JSON. A read succeeds only when the requested type agrees with the stored type; otherwise it reports a type mismatch.
storage_model.numeric_storage_rule: Numeric authored values use native numeric payloads as authoritative typed storage. Integers preserve signedness and width/range semantics, floating types preserve numeric floating payloads, and dimensioned/array numeric values preserve numeric element payloads. Decimal strings are permitted only at text/JSON serialization boundaries.

laws: semantic aliases are not foundational types, but each alias agrees with its underlying type
agreement is defined for alias-underlying and underlying-underlying comparisons, not for two semantic aliases
array element indexing over dimensioned arrays returns the entire dimensioned value
dictionary combine is closed and associative over dictionaries
when combining dictionaries, stronger non-dictionary values replace weaker values with the same key
when combining dictionaries, values with the same key are recursively combined only if both values are dictionaries
metadata fields may store underlying foundational types, but not semantic aliases
foundational raw values and durable values must not retain adapter JSON as their authoritative representation; tagged JSON is materialized on demand for adapters or serialization
raw and durable values have one observable stored type identity available for inspection without adapter JSON materialization

raw and durable values expose payload catalog metadata sufficient to distinguish scalar, dimensioned, matrix, quaternion, semantic alias, homogeneous array, dictionary, opaque, and valueBlock payload categories
half, float, double, and timecode values remain distinct typed payloads even if an implementation stores their bits in a common backend numeric container
integer scalar values remain distinct typed payloads for uchar, int, uint, int64, and uint64 even if an implementation stores them in a common backend numeric container

dimensioned, matrix, quaternion, and semantic alias values retain their declared type identity, element type, shape, and role metadata; they must not degrade to an untyped numeric vector for typed reads
arrays retain homogeneous element type metadata and element payload shape; they must not degrade to heterogeneous adapter arrays as durable storage
typed reads return the requested payload only when the requested type agrees with the stored type; otherwise they report a type mismatch
numeric typed values are not stored as decimal strings in raw or durable value payloads
the contract does not prescribe the physical storage layout for typed value payloads
